full_prompt: Design a model rocket airframe and internal structure for an amateur model-rocket accuracy contest. The rocket uses a commercially available solid motor in impulse class A, B, or C (select one class for the submission; this prompt targets class B: peak thrust 2-5 N, total impulse 2.5-5.0 N*s, typical specific impulse 70 s). The recovery subassembly mass (nose cone + parachute pack + recovery electronics + CanSat bay) must not exceed 500 g. The body tube inner diameter must accommodate a standard 350 ml CanSat form factor (66 mm OD) in its payload bay with at least 1 mm radial clearance; typical body tube ID 68 mm. The vehicle must be aerodynamically stable on the launch rail with a separation between center of gravity (CG) and center of pressure (CP) of at least 1 caliber (one body diameter) and not more than 2.5 calibers, measured with the motor installed. The altitude target for Class B is a pre-declared maximum altitude and the rocket is scored on how closely it achieves this target (accuracy contest). Structural pass/fail: the airframe must survive 20 G axial acceleration during motor burn and 10 G lateral gust loading. Under a 20 G axial quasi-static load applied at the motor mount, the body tube must not buckle and internal bulkheads must not deflect more than 1 mm. The fin root attachment must not fail under a 50 N side force at the tip. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static) plus a linear buckling eigenvalue check of the body tube under axial thrust load with load factor ≥ 1.5. Aerodynamic stability caliber margin is verified by Barrowman / RASAero-style CP analysis, which is a non-FEA solver. Vibration, thermal, fluid (CFD), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the airframe assembly, a mass budget, and a report listing recovery subassembly mass in g, CG-CP separation in calibers, body tube buckling load factor at 20 G axial, fin root max stress under 50 N side load in MPa.
short_prompt:
Design the airframe and internal structure of a Class B amateur model rocket for a model-rocket accuracy contest (peak thrust 2-5 N, total impulse 2.5-5.0 N*s). The body tube ID is ~68 mm to host a 350 ml CanSat (66 mm OD with >= 1 mm radial clearance); recovery subassembly (nose + chute + electronics + CanSat bay) <= 500 g. Static aerodynamic stability margin between 1.0 and 2.5 calibers with motor installed. Body tube under 20 g axial must have a linear buckling load factor >= 1.5.

Load cases.
- LC1 motor-burn axial: 20 G.
- LC2 lateral gust: 10 G.
- LC3 fin-tip side load: 50 N at the fin tip.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Stability margin is computed from Barrowman equations or equivalent (RASAero, OpenRocket) — analytical, not FEA.

prompt.title: Class-B amateur model rocket airframe
prompt.brief: Design a class-B model rocket airframe that holds a 350 ml CanSat, has 1-2.5 caliber stability, and survives 20 G axial + 10 G lateral.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Aerodynamic stability margin; > 1 caliber minimum, < 2.5 prevents over-stability

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Not required at this design gate.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Short burn, no thermal spec in rulebook.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Drag handled by Barrowman, not CFD.
